dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4147 IS: Fair Prices for Local Businesses Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Murphy (for himself, Mr. Welch, Mr. Gallego, Mr. Fetterman, and Mr. Blumenthal) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To strengthen the prohibition on price discrimination under the Clayton Act, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Prices for Local Businesses Act.

subsection.text: The Clayton Act (15 U.S.C. 12 et seq.) is amended—

clause.text: by striking in commerce each place it appears and inserting in commerce or in any activity affecting commerce;
by striking commodities each place it appears and inserting products or services;
by inserting service provision, after sale,;
by striking goods, wares, or merchandise and inserting products or services;
by striking goods each place it appears and inserting products or services; and
by inserting functional discounts or after due allowance for;
by inserting including a person charged with inducing or receiving such discrimination, after person charged with a violation of this section,; and

by striking : Provided, however, That nothing herein contained shall prevent a seller rebutting the prima-facie case thus made by showing that his lower price or the furnishing of services or facilities to any purchaser or purchasers was made in good faith to meet an equally low price of a competitor, or the services or facilities furnished by a competitor;
by striking in commerce and inserting in commerce or in any activity affecting commerce; and
by striking goods, wares, or merchandise and inserting products or services;
by striking in commerce and inserting in commerce or in any activity affecting commerce; and
by striking products or commodities each place it appears and inserting products or services;
by inserting engaged in commerce or in any activity affecting commerce after any person; and
by striking commodity each place it appears and inserting product or service;

subparagraph.text: by amending subsection (f) to read as follows:

paragraph.text: Subject to paragraph (2), it shall be unlawful for any person engaged in commerce or in any activity affecting commerce, in the course of such commerce or in the course of any activity affecting commerce, to induce or receive the benefit of any violation of this section.

In the case of a person with annual retail sales that do not exceed $100,000,000,000, paragraph (1) shall only apply if the person knowingly induced or received the benefit of the violation of this section.

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: the term purchase means to pay or grant anything of value in exchange for a product or service; and
the term purchaser means a person who pays or grants anything of value in exchange for a product or service, whether or not—

subparagraph.text: title passes to the payor or grantor; and
the payor or grantor exercises dominion or control over the product or service.
in subsection (a), by inserting and (c) after Except as provided in subsection (b);
by redesignating subsection (c) as subsection (d); and
by inserting after subsection (b) the following:

subsection.text: In an action brought with respect to a violation of any subsection of section 2, the plaintiff, upon a showing of proof that the plaintiff has been unlawfully discriminated against by the defendant—

paragraph.text: shall conclusively be presumed to have sustained injury and damages equal to the monetary amount or equivalent of the unlawful discrimination; and
may establish damages in addition to the damages described in paragraph (1), if any, that the plaintiff sustained as a result of the discrimination.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this Act shall apply to transactions occurring on or after the date of enactment of this Act.
